raw_response: \boxed{D} Based on the evidence reviewed, there is no information supporting either statement A ("center orientation of hydroxyapatite crystal is 0 degree") or statement B ("orientation of hydroxyapatite crystals at periphery is 45 degree"). The available literature describes the alignment of hydroxyapatite crystals in enamel rods following an undulating path from the enamel-dentine junction toward the outer occlusal surface, becoming more parallel at the periphery. It does not specify any exact angular measurements like zero degrees or forty-five degrees for central or peripheral orientations. Therefore, neither option A nor B can be confirmed as true according to the given data.
